query: length of Amazon River in kilometres
answer: The Amazon River is approximately 6,400 km long, but estimates vary from 6,575 to 7,062 km. It is the world's largest river by volume. The exact length is debated.

results.title: Amazon River - Wikipedia
results.snippet: It has a recognized length of 6,400 kilometers (4,000 mi), but according to some reports, its length varies from 6,575–7,062 kilometers (4,086–4,388 mi).

results.title: Spanning over 6400 km, the Amazon River is the world's ... - Instagram
results.snippet: Spanning over 6400 km, the Amazon River is the world's largest river in terms of sheer volume of water and the second longest river after the Nile.

results.title: How Long Is the Amazon River? | Britannica
results.snippet: It is the largest river by volume, and its basin is home to the Amazon Rainforest, the world’s richest and most-varied biological reservoir. Most scientists believe the South American river is at least 4,000 miles (6,400 km) long—still shorter than the Nile, which is widely held to be the world’s longest river at about 4,132 miles (6,650 km). In fact, even the definition of what constitutes a source has been debated—though most scientists agree it is the farthest point of continuously flowing water that can travel to the river’s mouth. Concerning its end point, the Amazon has three main outlets to the Atlantic Ocean: two on the northern side of Marajó Island in Brazil and one to the island’s south that joins the Pará River. They determined that Apacheta Creek—which is 6 miles (10 km) longer than the nearby Carhuasanta—is actually the Amazon’s source. In the end, the Amazon River’s total length was found to be 4,345 miles (6,992 km).

results.title: WATERS Amazon Basin
results.snippet: ## Rivers of the Amazon Basin. ### The farthest headwaters of the Amazon’s largest rivers range from approximately 2,500–6,800 km from the Amazon River mouth. Most debate on the farther headwater centers on what stream in the Ucayali Basin is farthest from the Amazon River mouth and on the exact location of the mouth of the Amazon River. The most distant headwaters of the Amazon River are located almost 6,100 kilometers from the Atlantic after considering the distances following the winding course of the great canals and tributaries. The aim of these expeditions is usually to determine if the Amazon River is longer than the Nile. There are plenty of estimates of the length of the Amazon River, ranging from 6,000 to 7,800 km. Amazon River headwaters of the Ucayali, in downstream order, are the Apurimac, the Ene, and the Tambo. The Amazon is the only river basin in the world that has three tributaries (the Purus, the Juruá and the Madeira) longer than 3,000 km.
